evidence: Ralph Townsend was an American author, consul and political activist noted for his opposition to the entry of the United States into World War II.

The Wars of the Delian League (477 – 449 BC) were a series of campaigns fought between the Delian League of Athens and her allies, and the Achaemenid Empire of Persia.
These conflicts represent a continuation of the Greco-Persian Wars, after the Ionian Revolt and the first and second Persian invasions of Greece.
Athens would gradually assume a more hegemonic position over the league, which gradually evolved into the Athenian Empire.
The grey reef shark (Carcharhinus amblyrhynchos) is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae.

As a result, the International Union for Conservation of Nature has assessed this species as Near Threatened.
Iowa Highway 160 (Iowa 160) is a short state highway that runs east and west in central Iowa.

Iowa Highway 160 was designated in 1947 as a diagonal connector route from Iowa Highway 60 to U.S. Route 69.
'On the Pulse of Morning' is a poem by African-American writer and poet Maya Angelou that she read at the first inauguration of President Bill Clinton.
The poem's themes are change, inclusion, responsibility, and role of both the President and the citizenry in establishing economic security.
With her public recitation, Angelou became the second poet in history to read a poem at a presidential inauguration, and the first African American and woman.

The Exchequer of Pleas or Court of Exchequer was a court that dealt with matters of equity.

Originally part of the curia regis, the Exchequer of Pleas split from the curia during the 1190s, to sit as an independent, central court.

Murrays' Mills is a complex of former cotton mills on land between Jersey Street and the Rochdale Canal.

The mill complex began to decline in the early 20th century.
The meat ant (Iridomyrmex purpureus) is a species of ant endemic to Australia.
It is characterised by its dark-bluish body and red head.
Meat ants establish territorial borders with neighbouring colonies and solve disputes through ritualised fighting.
Colonies range in size, varying between 11,000 individuals to over 300,000.

The International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP) is a two-year educational programme primarily aimed at students aged 16–19.
The program provides an internationally accepted qualification for entry into higher education.
Students complete assessments in six subjects, one from each subject group, and three core requirements.
Courses finish with an externally assessed series of examinations.
Elizabeth Fries Lummis Ellet was an American writer, historian and poet.

Saint Louis was the last of the three Charlemagne-class pre-dreadnought battleships built for the French Navy.

Satan's Harvest Home is a pamphlet published anonymously in 1749 in London.

The pamphlet discerns 'Reasons for the Growth of Sodomy' in a number of aspects of British culture.
It claims that contemporary infants faced grim prospects, being pampered in nurseries by mothers.
Dubh Artach is a remote skerry of basalt rock off the west coast of Scotland.

The rock is subject to extraordinary sea conditions with waves of 92 feet or more being encountered by the keepers.
Ashot Nadanian is an Armenian chess International Master (1997).

Two opening variations are named after him: the Nadanian Variation in the Grünfeld Defence and the Nadanian Attack in the Queen's Pawn Opening.
He has been described as a 'brilliant eccentric' and the 'Armenian Tal'.

Zero: Tsukihami no Kamen is a survival horror video game developed by Tecmo Koei.
The story, set on the fictional Rougetsu Island, focuses on Ruka Minazuki.
The gameplay revolves around the main character exploring environments and tackling hostile ghosts using the Camera Obscura.
The idea for Mask of the Lunar Eclipse came to series co-creator Keisuke Kikuchi when he first saw the Wii hardware.
An Elephant Makes Love to a Pig is the fifth episode in the first season of the American animated television series South Park.
The episode served as both a parody of genetic engineering and a statement against its potential evils.
The episode was met with generally positive reviews and has been described as one of the most popular early South Park episodes.
Several commentators praised its satirical elements with regard to genetic engineering.
Run-Away is a song by Super Furry Animals and the second single taken from their 2007 album, Hey Venus!
The song is an homage to the 'Wall of Sound' production made famous by Phil Spector.
The song received many positive reviews from critics who variously described it as 'absolutely timeless stuff'.
A 'spooky', 'tongue-in-cheek' video was produced for the song directed by Richard Ayoade.
Suillus americanus is a species of fungus in the Suillaceae family of mushrooms.
The mushroom can be recognized by the bright yellow cap with red to reddish-brown scales.
Suillus americanus was first described scientifically by American mycologist Charles Horton Peck in 1888.
In 1931, French mycologist Édouard-Jean Gilbert transferred the species to the genus Ixocomus.
Alfonsina Orsini was a Regent of Florence.
She governed the Republic of Florence during the absences of her son in the period of 1515 – 1519.
She helped restore the Medicis to power after they had been exiled.
She was also influential at the court of Pope Leo X, her brother-in-law.
Discipline Global Mobile is an independent record label founded in 1992 by Robert Fripp.
DGM has released solo music by Fripp as well as work by various affiliated musicians and bands.
DGM has aimed to be 'a model of ethical business in an industry founded on exploitation.'
Its policy is that its artists retain all copyrights.
M-125 is a 19 1/2-mile-long state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan.
The highway is entirely within Monroe County with the southern terminus on the Ohio state border near Toledo.
M-125 was first added to the state highway system in 1926 and given its current number in 1973.
The current designation was created in 1973 when US 25 was decommissioned in Michigan.

Oxford won by two-and-three-quarter lengths in a time of 18 minutes 29 seconds.

They have raised over US $ 100 million for these and other charitable causes.

evidence: 'Traveling' is a song recorded by Japanese–American recording artist Utada Hikaru.
The song received positive reviews from most music critics.
Musically, 'Traveling' is a dance-pop song, influenced by house music.
The song included live instrumentation by Saito Mitsutaka, who played the bass guitar.
It was also successful in Japan, peaking at number one on both the Oricon Singles Chart.
It was certified in two categories by the Recording Industry Association of Japan (RIAJ).
'Orison' is the seventh episode of the seventh season of the science fiction television series The X-Files.
The episode is a 'Monster-of-the-Week' story, unconnected to the series' wider mythology.
'Orison' serves as a sequel, and brings closure, to the second season episode 'Irresistible'.
Several heavily criticized the final scene featuring Scully killing Pfaster, calling it a betrayal of characterization.
The Battle of Kings Mountain was a decisive victory in South Carolina for the Patriot militia over the Loyalist militia.
The battle took place on October 7, 1780.
Ferguson issued a challenge to the rebel militias to lay down their arms or suffer the consequences.
The surprising victory over the American Loyalist militia came after a string of rebel defeats.
Tropical Storm Frances caused extensive flooding in Mexico and Texas in September 1998.
The storm produced heavy rains across the Isthmus of Tehuantepec, Texas, western Louisiana and the Great Plains.
Flooding that killed over 200 people and caused $63 million in damage.
Damage totaled US $500 million (1998 dollars), mainly from flooding.
No. 1 Basic Flying Training School (No. 1 BFTS) was a flying training school of the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF).
It was established in December 1951 at RAAF Base Uranquinty, New South Wales.
Flying training in the RAAF was again expanded in 1951–52.
No. 1 BFTS moved to RAAF Base Point Cook, Victoria, in May 1958.
William Lindsay Brandon was a physician, state legislator, planter and military officer.
Brandon served with the Confederate States Army from 1861 until 1864.
He fought in several major battles, such as the Battle of Malvern Hill.
Brandon was promoted to the rank of brigadier general in June 1864.
Tonga competed in the 2011 Commonwealth Youth Games held in the British Crown Dependency of Isle of Man.
Their participation marked their second Commonwealth Youth Games appearance.
Heamasi Sekona won a bronze in the light heavyweight class of boxing without winning a single bout.
None of the rest three athletes advanced past the qualifying stages.
Craig Kieswetter is an English former cricketer who appeared in 71 matches for England between 2010 and 2013.
He was considered a one-day specialist.
He scored his first — and only — international century in his third match.
He was named as the man of the match as England won the final of the 2010 ICC World Twenty20.
Oroville Dam is an earthfill embankment dam on the Feather River east of the city of Oroville, California.
At 770 feet high, it is the tallest dam in the U.S.
Construction was initiated in 1961, and despite numerous difficulties encountered during its construction.
The dam has prevented large amounts of flood damage to the area – more than $1.3 billion between the years of 1987 and 1999.
Tom Weisner is the mayor of Aurora, Illinois, which is the second largest municipality in the state.
He won re-election on April 9, 2013.
In 2007, he was embroiled in an interstate advertising controversy.
His decision-making skills again made headlines outside of Illinois when a Planned Parenthood clinic's permits became an issue.
